round1_fixes_verified.evidence: Task 3 step 1 (line 32), Acceptance Criteria (line 85), Details (line 158), Context Files source (line 116) and destination (line 131) all include ModelHelperExt.kt. AICore strip is explicitly described.

findings.issue: DownloadRepository.kt uses Task? parameter type in 4 locations (interface downloadModel line 55, interface observerWorkerProgress line 66, implementation downloadModel line 97, implementation observerWorkerProgress line 164) and references task?.id at line 134. Task class is defined in Tasks.kt which Task 3 explicitly does NOT copy. No patch instruction in Task 3 addresses removal or replacement of Task? parameter from DownloadRepository interface and implementation. This will produce compile errors in :core-runtime.
findings.fix: Add a patch step in Task 3 for DownloadRepository.kt: remove Task? parameter from both downloadModel() and observerWorkerProgress() signatures (interface + implementation). Replace task?.id with empty string literal or remove the TASK_ID_TAG tagging entirely. The tag is used only for Gallery deep-link navigation which is not ported to Phase 1 anyway.

findings.issue: DownloadRepository.kt references R.string.notification_title_success (line 212), R.string.notification_content_success (lines 213, 244), R.string.notification_title_fail (line 243) — all via import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.R (line 41). In :core-runtime (an Android library module), there is no R class with these strings. Task 3 Firebase removal patch (step 3) removes firebaseAnalytics calls but leaves the R.string.* calls intact. No instruction exists in Task 3 to remove import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.R or replace R.string.* calls with hardcoded string literals. This is a compile-time blocker.
findings.fix: Add a patch step in Task 3 for DownloadRepository.kt: (a) remove import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.R; (b) replace R.string.notification_title_success with a hardcoded string e.g. "Download complete"; (c) replace R.string.notification_content_success.format(model.name) with "\"${model.name}\" downloaded"; (d) replace R.string.notification_title_fail with "Download failed". These strings are notification text visible only when app is backgrounded.

findings.issue: Task 3 Firebase patch (step 3) instructs to delete 'import com.google.firebase.analytics.FirebaseAnalytics' — but this import does not exist in DownloadRepository.kt. The actual Firebase-related imports are com.google.ai.edge.gallery.GalleryEvent (line 40) and com.google.ai.edge.gallery.firebaseAnalytics (line 42). The Gallery R import at line 41 is also Gallery-specific and not Firebase. Task 3 does not mention removing the GalleryEvent import or all uses of GalleryEvent.MODEL_DOWNLOAD.id (appears in all 3 removed logEvent call-sites as the event name argument — technically gone with the logEvent removal, but the import line 40 remains). Minor risk: after firebase removals, a dangling 'import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.GalleryEvent' remains if agent follows instructions literally.
findings.fix: Fix step 3 to say: remove 'import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.firebaseAnalytics', 'import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.GalleryEvent', and all three firebaseAnalytics?.logEvent(...) blocks. Remove also 'import com.google.ai.edge.gallery.R' (handled in separate finding above but belongs in this step).

findings.issue: DownloadRepository.sendNotification() contains Gallery deep-link URI strings 'com.google.ai.edge.gallery://global_model_manager' (line 299) and 'com.google.ai.edge.gallery://model/{taskId}/{modelName}' (line 307). code-research.md §11 (line 479) explicitly states these should be deleted and sendNotification simplified to launchIntentForPackage. Task 3 does not instruct removal of these Gallery URI strings. After package rename, these hardcoded Gallery URIs will remain in :core-runtime, causing incorrect deep-link intent targets at runtime (notifications would try to navigate to Gallery, not PhoneWrap).
findings.fix: Add a patch step in Task 3 for DownloadRepository.sendNotification(): simplify the intent construction block (lines 291-310) to always use context.packageManager.getLaunchIntentForPackage(context.packageName) ?: return, eliminating the taskId/modelName branching and all Gallery URI references. Phase 1 has no deep-link routing from notifications.

findings.issue: Task 3 step 3 describes Firebase lines to delete as 'примерные line-ranges: 175-178, 220-227, 257-263'. Actual line ranges in gallery-source are: ENQUEUED block lines 175-178 (correct), SUCCEEDED block lines 220-228 (includes closing paren on 228, not 227), FAILED block lines 257-265 (includes closing paren on 265, not 263). Minor off-by-one could cause agent to leave orphaned closing braces. The word 'примерные' (approximate) mitigates this but still adds friction.
findings.fix: Update line ranges to precise values or add instruction to verify by context (the three logEvent calls are always followed by bundleOf arguments) rather than relying on line numbers.
